_meta.description: Mock product reviews of competitor skincare brands. Optional exercise: prompt your AI to mine these for unmet needs and recurring complaints.
_meta.usage: claude "Read datasets/sample_reviews.json. Cluster the complaints into themes, rank them by frequency, and identify the top 3 unmet needs a new sustainable skincare brand could exploit."

reviews.title: What does 'clean' even mean?
reviews.text: The product is fine but the marketing is so vague. 'Clean' and 'plant-powered' tell me nothing. I want to know where the ingredients actually come from.

reviews.title: Nice but pricey
reviews.text: Works well on my sensitive skin. Wish they were more transparent about sourcing though — feels like I'm paying for the Instagram aesthetic.

reviews.title: Feels luxurious
reviews.text: Beautiful glass bottles and it feels like a ritual. Carbon-neutral shipping is a nice touch, honestly assumed everyone did that now.

reviews.title: Too expensive for what it is
reviews.text: $60 for a serum and I still can't tell if the sustainability claims are real. Pretty packaging isn't proof.

reviews.title: Good, not great
reviews.text: Decent product. The 1% for the planet thing is nice but I'd rather they told me exactly what's in it and where it came from.

reviews.title: Affordable and simple
reviews.text: Love the short ingredient list and the price. Packaging is recyclable which is good, but no info on carbon or sourcing.

reviews.title: Cheap for a reason
reviews.text: Short ingredient list is nice marketing but the products feel basic. No transparency about where anything is sourced.

reviews.title: Best value clean beauty
reviews.text: Great for the price. Would pay a little more if they proved the sustainability instead of just claiming recyclable packaging.

reviews.title: The sourcing story sold me
reviews.text: I love that they tell the story of where the botanicals are grown. Only wish I could verify it — it's a story, not data.

reviews.title: Expensive storytelling
reviews.text: Beautiful narrative about regenerative farms but $72 is steep and I can't actually check any of it. Trust me bro isn't enough.

reviews.title: Closest to what I want
reviews.text: This is the only brand even trying to talk about traceability. If they added a QR code with real supplier data I'd be a customer for life.

reviews.title: Refills are genius
reviews.text: The refill system cut my packaging waste massively and it's cheaper over time. Wish they said more about ingredients.

reviews.title: Great idea, so-so product
reviews.text: Love the refill concept. The formulas are just okay and there's zero info on ingredient sourcing.

reviews.title: Refill pouches feel wasteful too
reviews.text: Ironic that the 'eco' refill still comes in a plastic pouch. And I still don't know what's actually in the product.

reviews.title: Greenwashing
reviews.text: Called them out on social for vague claims and got a canned response. If a brand can't tell me exactly what's in it, I assume the worst.

reviews.title: Would switch for transparency
reviews.text: Good product. I'd leave for any brand that let me scan and see verified ingredient sourcing. That's the missing piece for me.

reviews.title: Fine for the basics
reviews.text: Does the job. Fragrance-free options are limited which is a problem for my sensitive skin.

reviews.title: Can't verify anything
reviews.text: Lovely words about farms and soil. Zero ability to check it. Feels like premium-priced marketing.

reviews.title: Subscription is convenient
reviews.text: Auto-refill on subscription is great. Would love a fragrance-free line and actual ingredient traceability.

reviews.title: Pretty but shallow
reviews.text: The branding is gorgeous. The substance is thin. Tell me where it comes from and I'll pay more, I promise.
